note: Saved to repository root — .geninsights/ directory requires manual creation.

rules.rule_name: Person Search Criteria — At Least One Field Required
rules.description: A person search is executed only when at least one search criterion is provided. The search evaluates fields in OR logic: last name (partial match), first name (partial match), ZIP code (exact match), city (partial match), street (partial match), or house number (partial match). An empty search form returns no results.
rules.conditions: User clicks 'Suchen' (Search) button
rules.actions: Iterate search_space dataset; push matching persons to search_result array. If no fields are filled, search_result remains empty.
rules.exceptions: All six searchable fields are optional but at least one must be truthy to match any record.

rules.rule_name: Person Search — Case-Insensitive Partial String Matching
rules.description: Text-based person search fields (last name, first name, city, street, house number) use case-insensitive substring matching via toLowerCase().indexOf(). ZIP code uses exact string equality instead of substring matching.
rules.conditions: Applied for every non-empty text field in the search form
rules.actions: toLowerCase().indexOf() >= 0 for name, first, ort, street, hausnr; exact equality (==) for zip
rules.exceptions: ZIP (PLZ) uses exact equality only — partial ZIP prefix search is not supported.

rules.rule_name: Person Selection — Single Active Selection
rules.description: Only one person from the search result table can be active/selected at a time. Clicking a row sets that record as the selected_result, replacing any previous selection. The Zahlungsempfänger table shows payment methods for the currently selected person.
rules.conditions: User clicks a row in the search results table
rules.actions: this.selected_result = item; the Zahlungsempfänger table re-renders with selected_result.zahlungsempfaenger
rules.exceptions: None

rules.rule_name: Payment Method Selection — Single Active Selection
rules.description: Only one Zahlungsempfänger (payment recipient / bank account) can be selected at a time. Clicking a payment row sets it as zahlungsempfaenger_selected. This determines which IBAN/BIC/Gültig-ab is transmitted to Allegro.
rules.conditions: User clicks a row in the Zahlungsempfänger table
rules.actions: this.zahlungsempfaenger_selected = zahlungsempfaenger
rules.exceptions: If no payment row is selected, zahlungsempfaenger_selected remains an empty string; sending to Allegro will include an empty payment object.

rules.rule_name: Transfer to Allegro — Person and Payment Data Combined
rules.description: When 'Nach ALLEGRO übernehmen' is clicked, the selected person record is deep-cloned, the selected Zahlungsempfänger is attached (replacing the full array), and the combined object is sent via WebSocket with target='textfield'.
rules.conditions: User clicks 'Nach ALLEGRO übernehmen' button
rules.actions: Deep-clone selected_result via JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(e)); replace zahlungsempfaenger with single selected entry; send via WebSocket as { target: 'textfield', content: obj_to_send }
rules.exceptions: If no person selected, empty object {} sent. If no payment selected, empty string sent as payment.
rules.code_example: obj_to_send.zahlungsempfaenger = this.zahlungsempfaenger_selected; this.socket.send(JSON.stringify({ target: target, content: obj_to_send }));

rules.rule_name: Textarea Content — Real-Time Broadcast on Change
rules.description: Any change to the textarea field in the Vue.js web client is immediately broadcast to all connected Allegro clients via WebSocket with target='textarea'. This enables real-time sync of RT (Rechnungstext) notes to the Allegro desktop without a manual send action.
rules.conditions: Vue watcher detects any change to internal_content_textarea
rules.actions: this.sendMessage(val, 'textarea') — sends { target: 'textarea', content: text } via WebSocket
rules.exceptions: Allegro desktop does not send an acknowledgement.

rules.rule_name: WebSocket Message Routing — Target-Based Dispatch
rules.description: All WebSocket messages carry a 'target' field in a JSON envelope. The Allegro client dispatches based on target value: 'textarea' routes to the text area field; 'textfield' routes to individual person/payment form fields. Unknown target values are silently ignored.
rules.conditions: Any incoming WebSocket UTF-8 message
rules.actions: switch(message.target) { case 'textarea': populate textArea; case 'textfield': parse JSON and populate all form fields }
rules.exceptions: Messages with unrecognised target values fall through without action.
rules.code_example: switch (message.target) { case "textarea": textArea.setText(message.content); return; case "textfield": SearchResult sr = toSearchResult(message.content); ... }

rules.rule_name: WebSocket Relay — Broadcast to All Connected Clients
rules.description: The Node.js relay server broadcasts every received UTF-8 WebSocket message to ALL currently connected clients, including the sender. Pure broadcast — no filtering or point-to-point addressing.
rules.conditions: A UTF-8 WebSocket message is received from any client
rules.actions: for (var i = 0; i < clients.length; i++) { clients[i].sendUTF(json); }
rules.exceptions: Binary-type messages are silently ignored.
rules.code_example: if (message.type === 'utf8') { for (var i = 0; i < clients.length; i++) { clients[i].sendUTF(json); } }

rules.rule_name: WebSocket Connection Acceptance — No Origin Restriction (PoC Limitation)
rules.description: The Node.js relay server accepts WebSocket connections from any origin without restriction. request.accept(null, request.origin) with null origin means all cross-origin connections are permitted. Known PoC limitation.
rules.conditions: Any WebSocket upgrade request arrives at port 1337
rules.actions: Accept connection unconditionally: request.accept(null, request.origin)
rules.exceptions: No exceptions — all origins accepted. Must be restricted in production.
rules.code_example:
// later we maybe allow cross-origin requests
var connection = request.accept(null, request.origin);

rules.rule_name: Gender Field — Mandatory with Exactly Three Legal Options
rules.description: Gender (Geschlecht) is mandatory with three legally recognised German social insurance options: Weiblich (female), Männlich (male), Divers (diverse/non-binary). Represented as a mutually exclusive radio button group. Weiblich is the default on form load and after action reset.
rules.conditions: Always — one option is always selected
rules.actions: ButtonGroup enforces mutual exclusion. Default: rb_female.setSelected(true)
rules.exceptions: No 'unspecified' option available.

rules.rule_name: Post-Action Form Reset — Clear Fields After Submission
rules.description: After an action result (success or failure) is received via EventEmitter, the MVP presenter clears all form fields and resets gender to Weiblich default. The response body or 'Failed operation' error is displayed in the text area.
rules.conditions: EventEmitter emits any response after action() completes
rules.actions: Set textArea to response body; clear firstName, name, dateOfBirth, zip, ort, street, iban, bic, validFrom; reset female=true, male=false, diverse=false
rules.exceptions: Same reset runs regardless of success or failure.

rules.rule_name: HTTP POST Action — All Model Fields Submitted Together
rules.description: When 'Anordnen' is clicked in the MVP Swing client, ALL 13 ModelProperties enum values are serialised into a single JSON POST request. No partial submission. Fields with null values are stringified as 'null'.
rules.conditions: User clicks 'Anordnen' button
rules.actions: Build HashMap from all ModelProperties; POST to http://localhost:8080/post as application/json; emit response body
rules.exceptions: If HTTP response body is empty, emits 'Failed operation'. No retry logic.
rules.code_example: for(var val : ModelProperties.values()) { data.put(val.toString(), model.get(val).getField().toString()); } var responseBody = httpBinService.post(data);

rules.rule_name: Data Transfer Schema — 13 Standard Insurance Fields
rules.description: The canonical data schema for person and payment transfer consists of exactly 13 fields: TEXT_AREA, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH, ZIP, ORT, STREET, IBAN, BIC, VALID_FROM, FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE. Gap: legacy SearchResult includes hausnr (Hausnummer) absent from MVP enum and OpenAPI spec.
rules.conditions: Always — defines the contract for all data exchanges
rules.actions: All 13 fields included in model map and HTTP POST payload
rules.exceptions: Hausnummer exists in legacy path but is absent from MVP and OpenAPI spec.
rules.code_example: public enum ModelProperties { TEXT_AREA, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH, ZIP, ORT, STREET, IBAN, BIC, VALID_FROM, FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE }

rules.rule_name: Payment Validity Date — Gültig ab (Valid From)
rules.description: Each Zahlungsempfänger has a 'Gültig ab' (valid from) date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) indicating when that bank account became active. A valid_until counterpart exists in the data model (always empty) suggesting a planned lifecycle with start and end dates.
rules.conditions: Each Zahlungsempfänger record must carry a valid_from date
rules.actions: Displayed in Gültig-ab column; transmitted to Allegro as part of payment selection
rules.exceptions: valid_until is always empty string — expiry validation not yet implemented.

rules.rule_name: Person Record — Mandatory Identity Fields
rules.description: Every person record must carry: first name (Vorname), last name (Name), date of birth (Geburtsdatum), and a unique customer number (Kundennummer/knr). Address fields (PLZ, Ort, Strasse, Hausnummer) are present in all mock records. Extended reference fields (RV-Nummer, BG-Nummer, etc.) are displayed but disabled.
rules.conditions: All person records in the system
rules.actions: All mock records contain all identity fields; reference fields are display-only
rules.exceptions: Extended fields (nationality, Postfach, Träger-Nr.) are visible but disabled in the search form.
rules.code_example: {first:'Hans', name:'Mayer', dob:'1981-01-08', zip:'95183', ort:'Trogen', street:'Isaaer Str.', hausnr:'23', knr:'79423984', zahlungsempfaenger:[...]}

rules.rule_name: Person May Have Multiple Payment Methods
rules.description: A single insured person can have one or more Zahlungsempfänger entries (1..N). Mock dataset shows 1-3 payment methods per person, modelling real-world bank account changes over time.
rules.conditions: When a person is selected, all their payment methods are displayed
rules.actions: selected_result.zahlungsempfaenger array drives the payment selection table
rules.exceptions: A person with no zahlungsempfaenger entries would render an empty payment table.

rules.rule_name: Allegro Window — Fixed Title and Dimensions
rules.description: The Allegro desktop window is always titled 'Allegro' and has a fixed initial size of 800x650 pixels. The frame exits the JVM on close (EXIT_ON_CLOSE). Matches the branding and windowing of the legacy Allegro application.
rules.conditions: Application startup
rules.actions: JFrame title = 'Allegro'; setSize(800, 650); setDefaultCloseOperation(EXIT_ON_CLOSE); setVisible(true)
rules.exceptions: None
rules.code_example: private static JFrame frame = new JFrame("Allegro"); frame.setSize(800, 650);

rules.rule_name: Primary Action Button — Anordnen (Arrange/Commit)
rules.description: The primary action button is labelled 'Anordnen' (Arrange/Commit). In legacy implementation, it is a no-op placeholder. In MVP implementation, it triggers model.action() which POSTs all form data to HTTPBin service. Represents committing a benefit payment arrangement in Allegro.
rules.conditions: User clicks the 'Anordnen' button
rules.actions: Legacy: prints 'Button clicked!' to console. MVP: model.action() → HTTP POST → EventEmitter → form reset.
rules.exceptions: Legacy implementation is a no-op placeholder.

rules.rule_name: RT Field — Rechnungstext Free-Text Note Area
rules.description: The 'RT' field (ModelProperties.TEXT_AREA) is a multi-line free-text area for Rechnungstext (billing/invoice text). Updated via 'textarea' WebSocket messages or direct user input. Included in HTTP POST as TEXT_AREA key.
rules.conditions: Always visible; updated via WebSocket textarea target or direct input
rules.actions: textArea.setText(content) on incoming textarea message; included in HTTP POST as TEXT_AREA key
rules.exceptions: None

rules.rule_name: Disabled Fields — Read-Only Reference Data in Search Form
rules.description: Several fields in the Vue.js search form are explicitly disabled (HTML disabled): Kundennummer, IBAN (search form), BIC (search form), Betriebsbez., RV-Nummer, BG-Nummer, Träger-Nr. der gE. These reference fields cannot be manually edited.
rules.conditions: Always — permanently read-only in the search form
rules.actions: Fields accept no user input
rules.exceptions: IBAN and BIC are editable in the Allegro desktop form (not in search filter form).

rules.rule_name: Fixed Hardcoded Endpoint Configuration
rules.description: All service endpoints are hardcoded with no external configuration: WebSocket relay at ws://localhost:1337/ (all clients), HTTPBin service at http://localhost:8080/post (MVP model), relay server port 1337 (Node.js server). PoC limitation — production requires externalised configuration.
rules.conditions: Application startup
rules.actions: Connect to hardcoded endpoints
rules.exceptions: PoC limitation — no runtime configuration mechanism exists.
rules.code_example:
String uri = "ws://localhost:1337/";
public static final String URL = "http://localhost:8080";

rules.rule_name: Empty Response Fallback — 'Failed operation' Error Signal
rules.description: In PocModel.action(), if the HTTP response body is empty, the system emits 'Failed operation' via EventEmitter. The Presenter still executes a full form reset; the error is displayed in the text area.
rules.conditions: When httpBinService.post(data) returns an empty string
rules.actions: eventEmitter.emit('Failed operation') — view shows error in text area and still resets all fields
rules.exceptions: No differentiation between network errors and genuinely empty server responses.

workflows.workflow_name: Person Search and Transfer to Allegro
workflows.description: Primary end-to-end workflow: case worker searches for an insured person in the Vue.js web UI, selects person and payment method, transfers all data to the legacy Allegro desktop via WebSocket relay.
workflows.trigger: Case worker opens Vue.js web client and enters search criteria

steps.name: Enter Search Criteria
steps.description: Case worker enters one or more search criteria (last name, first name, ZIP, city, street, house number).

steps.name: Execute Search
steps.description: User clicks 'Suchen'. searchPerson() performs case-insensitive partial matching against the in-memory dataset.

steps.name: Review Search Results
steps.description: Case worker reviews the results table. If no results, refines criteria.

steps.name: Select Person
steps.description: Case worker clicks a row to select the person. Zahlungsempfänger table populates.

steps.name: Select Payment Method
steps.description: Case worker selects the appropriate IBAN/BIC/Gültig-ab from the payment table.

steps.name: Transfer to Allegro
steps.description: Case worker clicks 'Nach ALLEGRO übernehmen'. Person + payment data sent via WebSocket.

steps.name: Relay Broadcast
steps.description: Node.js relay broadcasts message to all connected clients.

steps.name: Allegro Receives and Populates
steps.description: Allegro desktop receives textfield message, parses JSON, populates all form fields.

steps.name: Case Worker Commits in Allegro
steps.description: Case worker verifies pre-filled data and clicks 'Anordnen' to commit the benefit payment arrangement.

steps.name: Form Reset
steps.description: After successful submission, MVP Allegro form resets all fields, shows server response in text area.

end_states: Data successfully transferred and committed in Allegro — form reset
HTTP POST fails — 'Failed operation' shown, form still reset
No person selected — empty object sent to Allegro
No payment selected — empty payment data sent to Allegro

workflows.description: Case workers type free-text RT (Rechnungstext) notes in the Vue.js web client. Notes broadcast in real-time to all connected Allegro desktops — no manual send required.
workflows.trigger: Case worker types or modifies content in the Vue.js textarea field

steps.name: Type in Textarea
steps.description: Case worker types content into the textarea in the Vue.js UI.

steps.name: Vue Watcher Triggers Send
steps.description: Vue watch on internal_content_textarea detects change and calls sendMessage(val, 'textarea').

steps.name: Relay Broadcast
steps.description: Node.js relay broadcasts { target: 'textarea', content: text } to all connected clients.

steps.name: Allegro Textarea Updated
steps.description: Allegro desktop receives message, identifies target='textarea', updates RT text area.

end_states: Allegro RT text area updated with latest note content

workflows.description: After form data is populated, the case worker commits via 'Anordnen', triggering HTTP POST to backend service and a form reset.
workflows.trigger: Case worker clicks 'Anordnen' button in MVP Allegro desktop

steps.name: Collect All Model Fields
steps.description: PocModel.action() iterates all 13 ModelProperties and builds key-value map.

steps.name: HTTP POST to HTTPBin
steps.description: All fields serialised as JSON and POSTed to http://localhost:8080/post.

steps.name: Evaluate Response
steps.description: If non-empty response, emit body. If empty, emit 'Failed operation'.

steps.name: Reset Form
steps.description: EventEmitter callback clears all text fields, resets gender to Weiblich, shows response/error in text area.

end_states: Form reset with server response shown in text area (success)
Form reset with 'Failed operation' shown in text area (empty response)

identified_gaps.description: No input validation before transfer — sending to Allegro with no person or payment selected sends empty objects.
identified_gaps.recommendation: Require both person and payment selection before enabling 'Nach ALLEGRO übernehmen' button.

identified_gaps.description: No authentication or authorisation on WebSocket relay (BR-009). All origins accepted.
identified_gaps.recommendation: Implement token-based WebSocket authentication and origin whitelisting for production.

identified_gaps.description: Hausnummer (house number) lost in MVP path — present in legacy SearchResult but absent from ModelProperties enum and OpenAPI spec.
identified_gaps.recommendation: Add HOUSE_NUMBER to ModelProperties enum and update OpenAPI spec.

identified_gaps.description: Payment validity not enforced — valid_until field exists but is never checked. Expired payment methods are displayed.
identified_gaps.recommendation: Filter payment methods by valid_from <= today <= valid_until (or no valid_until).

identified_gaps.description: Gender not set from incoming WebSocket textfield data — personal data populates form but gender fields remain at default.
identified_gaps.recommendation: Extend toSearchResult() parsing to include gender data from incoming message.

identified_gaps.description: Type inconsistency: OpenAPI spec declares FEMALE/MALE/DIVERSE as string; Java model uses Boolean.
identified_gaps.recommendation: Standardise to a single gender enum string or explicitly document the Boolean-to-string serialisation.

identified_gaps.description: Static mock dataset in Vue.js — person search operates against hardcoded in-memory data.
identified_gaps.recommendation: Replace static search_space with real backend API calls.
